turns.content: Quick recap of yesterday's supply-chain cleanup: I migrated all HTTP calls from axios to native fetch, dropped postinstall scripts repo-wide via ignore-scripts=true in .npmrc, and added the banned-http-client-library Semgrep rule. Anything I missed?

That covers the three big surfaces: runtime dependency, install-time execution, and CI gate. I'd add: refresh the rebuild:native allowlist in each repo's package.json so trusted native modules (better-sqlite3, esbuild, sharp) still compile, and document the rebuild step in CLAUDE.md so contributors don't get tripped up.

query: What HTTP client does the user use now?
